rules.rule: Each accordion header control is a native `<button>` (or `role="button"` only when a native button cannot be used).

rules.rule: The panel is shown/hidden in the DOM (e.g., via the `hidden` attribute), so that hidden content **cannot** be accessed by screen readers.
rules.static_hint: Collapse mechanism uses `hidden`, conditional rendering, or display:none — not opacity/height tricks that leave content in the accessibility tree.
rules.runtime_check: With a panel collapsed, assert its content is not visible, not focusable via Tab, and absent from the accessibility tree.

rules.rule: Users move focus between accordion headers using Tab / Shift+Tab.
rules.runtime_check: Tab and Shift+Tab through the accordion; assert each header button is reached in document order with no arrow-key-only traversal required.

rules.rule: Since the header control is a button, it is activated with Enter or Space.
rules.runtime_check: Focus a header button and press Enter, then Space; assert each toggles the panel's expanded state.

rules.rule: If the accordion does not permit a panel to be collapsed while expanded, the expanded header button uses `aria-disabled="true"` (rare case).
rules.static_hint: If the implementation enforces always-one-open, code sets aria-disabled="true" on the expanded header (not native disabled).
rules.runtime_check: Expand a panel and attempt to collapse it via its own header; if collapse is refused, assert that header carries aria-disabled="true" and remains focusable.

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the header controls.

rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus each header button; assert a visible focus indicator renders (computed outline/box-shadow change on focus).

rules.runtime_check: Assert the label is rendered visible (not sr-only or hidden).

rules.rule: Set `aria-expanded="true|false"` on the input reflecting whether the listbox is displayed.
rules.static_hint: The input carries aria-expanded bound to popup state.
rules.runtime_check: Open and close the listbox; assert aria-expanded always matches actual listbox visibility.

rules.rule: Set `aria-controls="IDREF"` on the input pointing to the listbox element.
rules.static_hint: The input's aria-controls IDREF resolves to the role="listbox" element.

rules.rule: The listbox is shown/hidden in the DOM (e.g., via the `hidden` attribute), so that when closed, its options cannot be reached by keyboard or screen readers.
rules.static_hint: Closed state uses `hidden`/conditional rendering/display:none, not visibility tricks that leave options exposed.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox closed, assert options are absent from the accessibility tree and unreachable.

rules.rule: Arrow Down moves to the first option, opening the listbox first if it is closed.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox closed, press Arrow Down in the input; assert the listbox opens and aria-activedescendant points to the first option.

rules.rule: Arrow Up moves to the last option, opening the listbox first if it is closed.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox closed, press Arrow Up; assert the listbox opens and aria-activedescendant points to the last option.

rules.rule: Alt+Arrow Down opens the listbox without moving the active option.
rules.runtime_check: Press Alt+Arrow Down with the listbox closed; assert the listbox opens and no aria-activedescendant is set (no option becomes active).

rules.rule: When the listbox is open, Arrow Up and Arrow Down move the active option and wrap at the ends.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox open, arrow through all options; assert aria-activedescendant advances each press and wraps last→first (Down) and first→last (Up).

rules.rule: Enter sets the input value to the active option and closes the listbox.
rules.runtime_check: Activate an option with Enter; assert the input's value equals that option's text and the listbox closes.

rules.rule: Home, End, Left, and Right move the caret within the input text rather than the active option.
rules.runtime_check: With text in the input and the listbox open, press Home/End/Left/Right; assert selectionStart changes appropriately and aria-activedescendant does not move.

rules.rule: A printable character returns focus to the input, inserts the character, and filters the listbox to matching options.
rules.runtime_check: Type a character while navigating options; assert it appears in the input value and the visible options reduce to matches.

rules.rule: Do not capture the keys the browser uses for text editing (character keys, caret movement, selection, and deletion).
rules.static_hint: Key handlers do not call preventDefault on printable characters, Backspace/Delete, Left/Right/Home/End, or Shift-selection combos.
rules.runtime_check: Type, delete, move the caret, and select text in the input; assert all native editing behaviors work unimpeded.

rules.rule: Keep DOM focus on the input at all times so native text editing keeps working.
rules.runtime_check: Open the listbox and navigate options; assert document.activeElement remains the input throughout.

rules.rule: Indicate the active option by setting `aria-activedescendant="{optionId}"` on the input, pointing to the currently highlighted option.
rules.static_hint: The input has aria-activedescendant bound to the active option's id.
rules.runtime_check: Arrow through options; assert aria-activedescendant always equals the id of the visually highlighted option.

rules.rule: When no option is active, remove `aria-activedescendant` (do not point it at a stale or nonexistent ID).
rules.runtime_check: Close the listbox or filter to zero matches; assert aria-activedescendant is absent (and never references an id missing from the DOM).

rules.rule: Scroll the active option into view via scripting when it changes so it is visible.
rules.runtime_check: With more options than fit the popup, arrow to a far option; assert its bounding box is within the listbox's visible scroll area.

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the input
rules.static_hint: No outline suppression on the input without :focus-visible replacement.
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus the input; assert a visible focus indicator renders.

rules.rule: Ensure a distinct visual highlight on the active option.
rules.runtime_check: Arrow through options; assert the active option's computed style visibly differs from inactive options (background/outline change).

rules.rule: Esc closes the listbox if it is open, or clears the input if the listbox is already closed.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox open, press Esc; assert it closes and the input value is retained. Press Esc again; assert the input value clears.

rules.rule: Do not capture the keys used for text editing (typing, caret movement, selection, deletion); the input must behave as a normal text field.
rules.static_hint: Flag preventDefault/stopPropagation on editing keys in the input's key handlers.
rules.runtime_check: Exercise typing, Backspace, caret movement, and Shift-selection in the input; assert all behave natively.

rules.rule: Do not move DOM focus into the options; it breaks native text editing. Track the active option with `aria-activedescendant` instead.
rules.static_hint: Flag .focus() calls on option elements or tabindex on options.
rules.runtime_check: Navigate options with arrows; assert document.activeElement never becomes an option element.

rules.rule: Do not use `role="menu"` or `role="menuitem"` for the options; use `role="listbox"` and `role="option"`.
rules.static_hint: Flag role="menu"/"menuitem" anywhere in the popup; require listbox/option.

rules.rule: Do not forget to scroll the active option into view when it changes.
rules.runtime_check: Arrow past the popup's visible area; assert the newly active option is scrolled into the visible region.

rules.rule: Do not leave the listbox visible while `aria-expanded="false"` (and vice versa).
rules.runtime_check: Across open/close cycles, assert listbox visibility and the input's aria-expanded value never disagree.

rules.rule: Use the native `<dialog>` element (preferred). When `<dialog>` cannot be used, `<div role="dialog">` is the documented fallback — see Customizable → Manual fallback.
rules.static_hint: Dialog surface element is a native <dialog>, or (fallback path) a <div> with role="dialog". Flag any other element/role combination.

rules.rule: Open with `.showModal()`. A `<dialog>` present in the DOM without `.showModal()` is non-modal — no focus trap, no background inertness, no `aria-modal`, no top-layer rendering.
rules.static_hint: AST contains a `.showModal()` call on the dialog ref/element in the open path; flag `.show()` or setting the `open` attribute directly.
rules.runtime_check: Activate the trigger; assert the dialog is open and matches the `:modal` pseudo-class (modal state, top layer).

rules.rule: Close with `.close()`. Unmounting a `<dialog>` while open (rather than calling `.close()`) leaves focus stranded because the browser's focus-restoration to the invoker runs on `.close()`. Both lifecycle endpoints must be exercised.
rules.static_hint: AST contains a `.close()` call in the close path; flag close handlers that only unmount/conditionally render the dialog away without calling `.close()` first.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dialog, activate the close control; assert the dialog is closed AND focus has returned to the invoking element (stranded focus on <body> indicates unmount-without-close).

rules.rule: Dialog surface has an accessible name via `aria-labelledby` (preferred) or `aria-label`.
rules.static_hint: Dialog element carries `aria-labelledby` or `aria-label` with a non-empty value.

rules.rule: If `aria-labelledby` is used, it references a visible title element (e.g., `<h2 id="...">`).
rules.static_hint: The `aria-labelledby` IDREF resolves to an element inside the dialog that is a heading/title element with a matching `id`.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, the referenced element exists in the DOM and is visible (not display:none, visibility:hidden, or zero-size).

rules.rule: If a description is rendered, it is referenced by `aria-describedby`. Do not rely on incidental reading order.
rules.static_hint: If `aria-describedby` is present, its IDREF resolves to an existing element inside the dialog.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the dialog render descriptive/explanatory text, and if so, is that text the element referenced by `aria-describedby` (rather than left to incidental reading order)?

rules.rule: Dialog width is fluid so content reflows at 400% zoom (WCAG 1.4.10 Reflow).
rules.static_hint: Dialog surface CSS uses a fluid width constraint (e.g., `max-width: min(<Npx>, 100%)` or percentage/viewport-relative width), not a fixed `width: <Npx>`.
rules.runtime_check: Set viewport to 320 CSS pixels wide (simulating 400% zoom), open the dialog; assert no horizontal scrolling and dialog content reflows within the viewport.

rules.rule: Prefer `max-width: min(<Npx>, 100%)`; do not set fixed pixel widths that would exceed the 320-CSS-pixel viewport at 400% zoom.
rules.static_hint: No `width` or `min-width` declaration on the dialog surface with a fixed pixel value > 320px; `max-width` uses the `min(<Npx>, 100%)` pattern or equivalent fluid constraint.

rules.rule: Provide a visible close control (`<button type="button">`).
rules.static_hint: Dialog contains a native <button> with `type="button"` serving as the close control.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, the close button is visible in the rendered layout (nonzero size, not hidden).

rules.rule: Close control has an accessible name (e.g., `aria-label="Close dialog"`).
rules.static_hint: The close button has a non-empty accessible name source: text content, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby` (flag icon-only buttons with none).
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the close button's accessible name convey the close action (e.g., "Close dialog") rather than being empty, generic, or describing only the icon (e.g., "X")?

rules.rule: The invoking control declares dialog-trigger semantics — apply `aria-haspopup="dialog"` to the trigger element.
rules.static_hint: An element carries `aria-haspopup="dialog"` (exact value "dialog", not "true").
rules.llm_eval_question: Is the element carrying `aria-haspopup="dialog"` the actual control whose activation opens the dialog?

rules.rule: Focus moves into the dialog on open.
rules.runtime_check: Activate the trigger; assert `document.activeElement` is inside the dialog (the dialog itself or a descendant).

rules.rule: Focus is trapped within the dialog while open.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, press Tab repeatedly past the last focusable element and Shift+Tab before the first; assert focus always remains within the dialog and never lands on background content.

rules.rule: Escape closes the dialog.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, press Escape; assert the dialog is closed.

rules.rule: Focus returns to the invoking element on close.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dialog from the trigger, close it (via close button and via Escape); assert `document.activeElement` is the original trigger element.

rules.rule: Background content is not focusable or reachable by keyboard or screen readers while open.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, attempt to Tab to background controls and programmatically `.focus()` a background element; assert focus cannot land there, and assert background content is excluded from the accessibility tree (inert or aria-hidden effective state).

rules.rule: Body scroll is prevented while open.
rules.runtime_check: On a page taller than the viewport, open the dialog and attempt to scroll (wheel/keyboard); assert `window.scrollY`/body scroll position does not change while the dialog is open.

rules.rule: Under native `<dialog>` + `.showModal()` (the Golden Pattern), these six behaviors are provided automatically by the browser. Under the manual `<div role="dialog">` fallback (Customizable → Manual fallback), each is the implementation's responsibility.
rules.static_hint: Detect which variant is in use: native <dialog> with `.showModal()` vs. `<div role="dialog">`. If the fallback variant, require presence of explicit implementations (focus trap logic, Escape handler, inert/isolation, scroll lock, focus restoration).
rules.runtime_check: For the manual fallback variant, run the full six-point behavior contract checks (focus-in on open, trap, Escape, focus return, background unreachable, scroll lock) — none are provided for free.

rules.rule: Focus indicators on the dialog surface, close button, and any focusable content follow the Foundations focus rule.
rules.static_hint: No CSS that suppresses focus visibility without replacement (e.g., `outline: none` / `outline: 0` on focusable elements with no `:focus-visible` styles).
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus the dialog surface, the close button, and each focusable descendant; assert a visible focus indicator is rendered on each (computed outline/box-shadow/border change on focus).

rules.rule: Do not render `<dialog>` without calling `.showModal()` and expect modal behavior. A bare `<dialog>` produces a non-modal reveal with none of the modal contract.
rules.static_hint: Flag a <dialog> opened via `.show()`, via the `open` attribute in markup, or via toggling `open` in code, when the pattern is presented as a modal.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dialog; assert it matches `:modal` and that background content is inert — a bare <dialog> fails both.

rules.rule: Do not implement the manual `<div role="dialog">` fallback without every one of its Must Haves. Partial implementations produce broken screen-reader announcements and stranded focus.
rules.static_hint: If `<div role="dialog">` is detected, require the full static checklist: `aria-modal`, accessible name, close button, aria-haspopup on trigger, no fixed widths, inert handling code present.
rules.llm_eval_question: Given this is the manual fallback, does the implementation address every Must Have from the ruleset, or are any silently missing?
rules.runtime_check: Run the complete six-point behavior contract plus name/description announcements against the fallback implementation; any single failure confirms a partial implementation.

rules.rule: Do not rely on `aria-modal="true"` to block background interaction; it is announcement metadata, not an interaction gate. Background isolation is a separate implementation concern.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the code implement a real background-isolation mechanism (inert on the app root, focus trap, or native `.showModal()`), or is `aria-modal="true"` the only thing standing between the user and the background?
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, Tab and programmatically focus background elements; assert they are unreachable — proving isolation holds independently of the `aria-modal` attribute.

rules.rule: Do not render the manual fallback inside containers that create clipping or stacking contexts (e.g., `overflow: hidden/auto`, `transform`).
rules.static_hint: Fallback dialog is rendered/portaled to <body> (or equivalent top-level node) rather than nested inside components whose styles declare `overflow: hidden/auto` or `transform`.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, walk its DOM ancestors and assert none have computed `overflow: hidden/auto/scroll` or a non-none `transform`; assert the dialog is fully visible and unclipped in the viewport.

rules.rule: Do not rely on `z-index` alone to "make it work."
rules.llm_eval_question: Is the dialog's layering strategy structural (top layer via `.showModal()`, or a portal to the document root), or is the implementation just stacking a large `z-index` on a nested element and hoping?
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, assert it is the topmost hit-testable element at its own coordinates (elementFromPoint over the dialog returns the dialog or a descendant) and is not obscured or clipped by other stacking contexts.

rules.rule: Do not inert `document.body` or `document.documentElement` on the manual fallback.
rules.static_hint: Flag code that sets `inert` (property or attribute) on `document.body` or `document.documentElement`.
rules.runtime_check: With the fallback dialog open, assert `document.body.inert` and `document.documentElement.inert` are false, and that the dialog's own controls remain focusable (inerting body would disable the dialog too).

rules.rule: Inert only the application content root.
rules.static_hint: Inert is applied to the app content root element (e.g., `#root` / `#app` / main content wrapper), a sibling of the dialog's mount point.
rules.runtime_check: With the fallback dialog open, assert the app content root has `inert` (its controls unfocusable and out of the accessibility tree) while the dialog subtree remains fully interactive.

rules.rule: Do not omit focus restoration; closing a dialog must return the user to the element that invoked it.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dialog from the trigger, close it by each supported path (close button, Escape); assert `document.activeElement` is the invoking element after every close path.

rules.rule: Do not set fixed pixel widths on the dialog surface that exceed the 320-CSS-pixel viewport at 400% zoom.
rules.static_hint: Flag `width`, `min-width`, or non-fluid `max-width` declarations on the dialog surface with fixed pixel values > 320px.
rules.runtime_check: At a 320-CSS-pixel-wide viewport, open the dialog; assert its rendered width does not exceed the viewport and no horizontal scrollbar appears.

rules.rule: The dialog surface uses `role="dialog"`, or a native `<dialog>` element shown non-modally (rendered in the DOM or opened with `.show()`, never `.showModal()`).
rules.static_hint: Surface is role="dialog" or a native <dialog> whose open path uses `.show()`/the `open` attribute; flag any `.showModal()` call.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dialog; assert it does NOT match the `:modal` pseudo-class and the page behind remains interactive.

rules.rule: The dialog has an accessible name via `aria-labelledby` (preferred), referencing a visible title element (e.g., `<h2 id="...">`), or `aria-label`.
rules.static_hint: Dialog surface carries a non-empty aria-label, or aria-labelledby whose IDREF resolves to a title element inside the dialog.
rules.runtime_check: If aria-labelledby is used, assert the referenced title element is rendered visible while the dialog is open.

rules.rule: Focus moves into the dialog on open, landing on the dialog container or its first interactive control.
rules.runtime_check: Activate the trigger; assert document.activeElement is the dialog container or its first interactive control.

rules.rule: Esc closes the dialog, regardless of where focus currently sits, since focus is not trapped and may have moved into the page.
rules.static_hint: Esc handler is bound at the document level, not only on the dialog surface.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dialog, Tab focus out into the page, press Esc; assert the dialog closes even though focus was outside it.

rules.rule: Focus is restored to the invoking element on close.
rules.runtime_check: Open from the trigger, close the dialog; assert document.activeElement is the original invoking element.

rules.rule: Capture the invoking element at open time, and restore focus with `requestAnimationFrame` after the dialog is unmounted.
rules.static_hint: Open handler stores document.activeElement (or the trigger ref); close path calls focus() on it inside requestAnimationFrame.
rules.runtime_check: Close the dialog; assert focus lands on the invoker on the frame after unmount (not stranded on <body> due to restoring before removal).

rules.rule: The dialog does not trap focus. Tab and Shift+Tab move out of the dialog into the rest of the page, which stays interactive.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, Tab past its last focusable element; assert focus moves into the page and page controls remain clickable/focusable.

rules.rule: The dialog does not set `aria-modal="true"`
rules.static_hint: No aria-modal="true" on the dialog surface.

rules.rule: The dialog does not apply `inert` to the background.
rules.static_hint: No code sets `inert` on the app root, body, or background containers when this dialog opens.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, assert background elements are focusable and have no inert ancestor.

rules.rule: Provide a visible close control. Use a native `<button>` (preferred), or `role="button"` only when a native button cannot be used.
rules.static_hint: Dialog contains a close control that is a native <button>, or role="button" as documented fallback.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, assert the close control is rendered visible.

rules.rule: If `role="button"` is used instead of a native `<button>`, add `tabindex="0"`
rules.static_hint: Every role="button" close control also carries tabindex="0".

rules.rule: If `role="button"` is used instead of a native `<button>`, add keyboard support for Enter and Space.
rules.static_hint: role="button" element has key handlers covering Enter and Space.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the role="button" close control, press Enter, then Space; assert each closes the dialog.

rules.rule: ensuring Space prevents page scrolling while activating the control
rules.runtime_check: On a scrollable page, press Space on the focused role="button" control; assert activation occurs and the page does not scroll.

rules.rule: The close control has an accessible name that describes its purpose or action (e.g., `aria-label="Close"`).
rules.static_hint: Close control has a non-empty accessible name source.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the close control's name convey the close action rather than being generic or icon-descriptive?

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the trigger, the close control, and any focusable content within the dialog.
rules.static_hint: No outline suppression without :focus-visible replacement on the trigger, close control, or dialog content.
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus the trigger, close control, and each focusable descendant; assert a visible focus indicator renders on each.

rules.rule: Do not set `aria-modal="true"` on a non-modal dialog; it falsely tells assistive technology the background is inert.
rules.static_hint: Flag aria-modal="true" on the non-modal dialog surface.

rules.rule: Do not trap focus within the dialog.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, Tab past its last focusable element; assert focus exits into the page instead of cycling back inside.

rules.rule: Do not apply `inert` to, or otherwise block, the background content.
rules.static_hint: Flag inert application, full-screen blocking overlays, or pointer-events:none on background containers tied to this dialog's open state.
rules.runtime_check: With the dialog open, click and focus background controls; assert they respond normally.

rules.rule: Do not omit focus restoration; closing the dialog must return focus to the element that invoked it.
rules.runtime_check: Close the dialog by each supported path; assert document.activeElement is the invoking element after each.

rules.rule: Do not bind Esc only to a container-level handler on the dialog surface; once focus leaves the untrapped dialog, that handler stops firing and Esc no longer closes.
rules.static_hint: Flag an Esc keydown handler attached only to the dialog element with no document-level listener.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dialog, move focus into the page outside it, press Esc; assert the dialog still closes.

rules.rule: Use a native `<button>` (preferred), or `role="button"` only when a native button cannot be used.
rules.static_hint: Trigger is a native <button>, or an element with role="button".
rules.llm_eval_question: If role="button" is used, is there a genuine constraint that prevented a native <button>?

rules.rule: If `role="button"` is used instead of a native `<button>`, add `tabindex="0"`
rules.static_hint: Every role="button" trigger also carries tabindex="0".

rules.rule: If `role="button"` is used instead of a native `<button>`, add keyboard support for Enter and Space.
rules.static_hint: role="button" trigger has key handlers covering Enter and Space.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the role="button" trigger, press Enter, then Space; assert each toggles the region.

rules.rule: ensuring Space prevents page scrolling while activating the control
rules.runtime_check: On a scrollable page, press Space on the focused role="button" trigger; assert activation occurs and the page does not scroll.

rules.rule: The button reflects open state with `aria-expanded="true|false"`.
rules.static_hint: Trigger carries aria-expanded bound to the region's open state.
rules.runtime_check: Toggle the disclosure; assert aria-expanded is "true" exactly when the region is visible and "false" when hidden.

rules.rule: The button references the region with `aria-controls="IDREF"`.
rules.static_hint: Trigger's aria-controls IDREF resolves to the region element's id.

rules.rule: The region is shown/hidden in the DOM (e.g., via the `hidden` attribute), so that when closed, its content cannot be reached by keyboard or screen readers.
rules.static_hint: Closed state uses `hidden`/conditional rendering/display:none, not visual-only hiding.
rules.runtime_check: With the region closed, assert its content is unreachable via Tab and absent from the accessibility tree.

rules.rule: The button has an accessible name that describes the content it controls.
rules.static_hint: Trigger has a non-empty accessible name source.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the trigger's name describe the disclosed content (e.g., "Shipping details") rather than being generic ("More", "Expand")?

rules.rule: When the button has visible text, the visible text serves as the accessible name.
rules.static_hint: No aria-label/aria-labelledby overriding the visible text on text-bearing triggers.
rules.runtime_check: Assert the computed accessible name matches the trigger's rendered visible text.

rules.rule: For an icon-only trigger, provide an accessible name using `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`.
rules.static_hint: Icon-only triggers carry a non-empty aria-label or resolving aria-labelledby.

rules.rule: The button omits `aria-haspopup`.
rules.static_hint: No aria-haspopup attribute on the disclosure trigger.

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the trigger.
rules.static_hint: No outline suppression without :focus-visible replacement on the trigger.
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus the trigger; assert a visible focus indicator renders.

rules.rule: Do not use `role="menu"` on the region.
rules.static_hint: Flag role="menu" on the disclosure region.

rules.rule: Do not add `aria-haspopup` to the button.
rules.static_hint: Flag any aria-haspopup value on the disclosure trigger.

rules.rule: Do not put the expanded state on the region instead of the trigger.
rules.static_hint: Flag aria-expanded on the region element; it belongs on the trigger button.

rules.rule: Do not leave the region visible while `aria-expanded="false"` (and vice versa).
rules.runtime_check: Across toggle cycles, assert region visibility and the trigger's aria-expanded value never disagree.

rules.rule: Use `role="grid"` with an accessible name (e.g., `aria-label="Channel guide"`).
rules.static_hint: Grid container carries role="grid" and a non-empty aria-label (or aria-labelledby).

rules.rule: Use `role="row"` for each row, including the header row.
rules.static_hint: Every row element, header row included, carries role="row".

rules.rule: Use `role="columnheader"` for the time header cells (typically static).
rules.static_hint: Time header cells carry role="columnheader".

rules.rule: Use `role="rowheader"` for the channel name/logo column (may be interactive).
rules.static_hint: First-column channel cells carry role="rowheader".

rules.rule: Use `role="gridcell"` for program listing cells (interactive).
rules.static_hint: Program cells carry role="gridcell".

rules.rule: Grid structural roles (`gridcell`, `rowheader`, `columnheader`) must be applied to container elements.
rules.static_hint: Structural roles sit on non-interactive container elements (div/span/td-like), never on <button>/<a>.

rules.rule: Interactive controls (e.g., `<button>`, `<a>`) must be nested inside those containers.
rules.static_hint: Each interactive cell contains a nested <button>/<a> as a child of the role-bearing container.

rules.rule: Set `aria-rowcount` to the total number of rows in the grid.
rules.static_hint: Grid element carries aria-rowcount computed from the full data set, not the rendered slice.
rules.runtime_check: With lazy rendering active, assert aria-rowcount equals the total logical row count, not the rendered row count.

rules.rule: Set `aria-colcount` to the total number of columns in the grid (including the channel column).
rules.static_hint: Grid element carries aria-colcount that counts the channel column plus all time columns.
rules.runtime_check: Assert aria-colcount equals the total logical column count including the channel column, regardless of how many columns are rendered.

rules.rule: Set `aria-rowindex` on each row (1-based, counting the header row), so position announcements remain correct when rows are rendered lazily.
rules.static_hint: Every role="row" carries a 1-based aria-rowindex; the header row is index 1.
rules.runtime_check: Scroll to lazily load additional rows; assert each rendered row's aria-rowindex reflects its position in the full set.

rules.rule: Set `aria-colindex` on each cell (1-based, counting the channel column), so position announcements remain correct when columns are rendered lazily.
rules.static_hint: Every cell carries a 1-based aria-colindex; the channel column is index 1.
rules.runtime_check: Scroll horizontally to load later time columns; assert each cell's aria-colindex reflects its global column position.

rules.rule: The grid must be a single Tab stop.
rules.runtime_check: Tab from before the grid; assert one press enters it and the next Tab press exits it — cells never consume additional Tab stops.

rules.rule: Only one cell is tabbable at a time (roving `tabIndex`: active cell `0`, all others `-1`).
rules.static_hint: Cell controls render tabIndex bound to active-cell state, defaulting to -1.
rules.runtime_check: At any moment, assert exactly one cell control has tabindex="0" and all others have tabindex="-1".

rules.rule: Arrow keys move focus within the grid (Left/Right/Up/Down).
rules.runtime_check: Focus a cell and press each arrow key; assert focus moves to the adjacent cell in the corresponding direction.

rules.rule: Tab/Shift+Tab exits the grid to the next/previous focusable element outside.
rules.runtime_check: With focus on any cell, press Tab and Shift+Tab; assert focus lands on the next/previous focusable element outside the grid.

rules.rule: Channel column (row header) opens "channel details".
rules.runtime_check: Activate a channel rowheader control (Enter/click); assert the channel details view opens.

rules.rule: "Now" column activates tune (no-op if already selected).
rules.runtime_check: Activate a Now-column cell; assert tune fires. Activate it again while selected; assert no state change or error.

rules.rule: Future columns open "program details" (demo can use a modal).

rules.rule:
Top-level navigation items are presented in a list structure (e.g., `
  • …
`).
rules.static_hint: Top-level items are <li> children of a <ul> with no role overrides stripping list semantics.

rules.rule: Each top-level item is one of the following: (A) Simple link: a single `<a>` to a destination. (B) Parent link + menu button (when the parent has its own destination page): the parent is a normal `<a>` (activates navigation), and a separate adjacent menu toggle button opens/closes the sub-menu. (C) Parent button (when the parent does not have its own destination page): a `<button>` acts as the parent control and opens/closes the sub-menu.
rules.static_hint: Each top-level <li> matches exactly one shape: lone <a>; <a> plus sibling toggle <button>; or lone <button> controlling a submenu.
rules.llm_eval_question: For each parent with a submenu, was case B vs. case C chosen correctly based on whether the parent has its own destination page?

rules.rule: The toggle control is a native `<button>` (preferred) or `role="button"` only when a native button cannot be used.
rules.static_hint: Submenu toggles are native <button> elements, or role="button" as documented fallback.
rules.llm_eval_question: If role="button" is used, is there a genuine constraint that prevented a native <button>?

rules.rule: If using `role="button"`, add `tabindex="0"`
rules.static_hint: Every role="button" toggle also carries tabindex="0".

rules.rule: If using `role="button"`, add keyboard support for Enter and Space.
rules.static_hint: role="button" toggle has key handlers covering Enter and Space.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the role="button" toggle, press Enter, then Space; assert each toggles the submenu.

rules.rule: ensuring Space prevents page scrolling while activating.
rules.runtime_check: On a scrollable page, press Space on the focused role="button" toggle; assert activation occurs and the page does not scroll.

rules.rule: The sub-menu container is positioned immediately after its toggle control in the DOM.
rules.static_hint: Each submenu element is the next sibling (or immediately follows) its toggle in source order.

rules.rule:
Sub-menu items are links and/or buttons contained in a list structure (`
  • …
`).
rules.static_hint: Submenu content is <a>/<button> elements inside <li> children of a <ul>.

rules.rule: If the toggle is an icon button associated with a parent link (case B), it has an accessible name that includes the parent link text.
rules.static_hint: Case-B toggle's aria-label contains the adjacent parent link's text (e.g., "Categories menu").
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the toggle's accessible name include the parent link's actual label so the pairing is announced?

rules.rule: If the top-level item is a link, apply aria-current="page" to that link.
rules.static_hint: aria-current="page" logic exists, bound to the current route matching a top-level link.
rules.runtime_check: Navigate to a page owned by a top-level link; assert that link (and only that link) carries aria-current="page".

rules.rule: If the top-level item is a button-only parent (no destination), do not put aria-current on the button. Instead, apply aria-current="page" to the submenu link that represents the current page.
rules.static_hint: No aria-current on toggle buttons; current-page logic targets submenu links.
rules.runtime_check: Navigate to a page under a button-only parent; assert the button has no aria-current and the matching submenu link carries aria-current="page".

rules.rule: If the navigation has no explicit Home link and the site logo links to the homepage, the logo link carries `aria-current="page"` when the user is on the homepage.
rules.static_hint: Logo-link component conditionally applies aria-current="page" on the home route.
rules.runtime_check: Load the homepage; assert the logo link carries aria-current="page", and loses it on other routes.

rules.rule: Ensure there is some visual change that indicates this is the current page link.
rules.runtime_check: Compare computed styles of the aria-current link against its siblings; assert a visible difference (underline, weight, color-plus-cue).

rules.rule: The toggle reflects open state with `aria-expanded="true|false"`.
rules.static_hint: Toggle carries aria-expanded bound to submenu state.
rules.runtime_check: Open and close each submenu; assert its toggle's aria-expanded always matches actual submenu visibility.

rules.rule: The toggle references its submenu with `aria-controls="IDREF"` (recommended).
rules.static_hint: Toggle's aria-controls IDREF resolves to its submenu container's id.

rules.rule: The toggle does not carry `aria-haspopup`; a submenu of links is not a menu, listbox, tree, grid, or dialog, so `aria-expanded` alone is the correct state signal.
rules.static_hint: No aria-haspopup attribute on submenu toggles.

rules.rule: Top-level items that open a sub-menu include a visible indicator (e.g., down caret) that communicates "has submenu."
rules.static_hint: Submenu-opening items render an indicator element (caret/chevron).
rules.runtime_check: Assert the indicator is rendered visible on each submenu-opening top-level item.

rules.rule: The indicator is decorative and does not replace the accessible name of the toggle (e.g., use an aria-label like "Categories menu" on icon-only toggles).
rules.static_hint: Indicator carries aria-hidden="true"; toggle retains its own accessible name.
rules.llm_eval_question: Is the toggle's name a real label rather than the indicator glyph (no "▼" or "chevron" as the accessible name)?

rules.rule: Sub-menus all default to closed, or hidden, when the page loads.
rules.static_hint: Initial state renders every submenu hidden (hidden attribute / not mounted).
rules.runtime_check: Load the page; assert no submenu is visible and every toggle has aria-expanded="false".

rules.rule: The sub-menu is shown/hidden in the DOM (e.g., via `hidden`) so that when closed, submenu links cannot be reached by keyboard or screen readers.
rules.static_hint: Closed state uses `hidden`/conditional rendering/display:none, not offscreen positioning.
rules.runtime_check: With a submenu closed, assert its links are unreachable via Tab and absent from the accessibility tree.

rules.rule: If focus is on the last focusable element in an open submenu and the user presses Tab, the submenu closes and focus moves to the next focusable element after the submenu.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the last submenu item, press Tab; assert the submenu closes and focus lands on the next focusable element after it.

rules.rule: If focus is on the first focusable element in an open submenu and the user presses Shift+Tab, the submenu closes and focus moves to the toggle control.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the first submenu item, press Shift+Tab; assert the submenu closes and focus lands on its toggle.

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around all links and buttons.
rules.static_hint: No outline suppression without :focus-visible replacement on nav links and toggles.
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus each nav link, toggle, and submenu item; assert a visible focus indicator renders on each.

rules.rule: The sub-menu is non-modal and does not trap focus.
rules.runtime_check: With a submenu open, Tab beyond its bounds; assert focus exits into the page rather than cycling within the submenu.

rules.rule: When a submenu is opened, focus remains on the toggle control.
rules.runtime_check: Activate a toggle; assert document.activeElement is still the toggle, not a submenu item.

rules.rule: Keyboard users reach the first submenu item with Tab.
rules.runtime_check: Open a submenu from its toggle, press Tab; assert focus lands on the first submenu item.

rules.rule: Users can Tab through submenu items and continue to the rest of the page (no focus trap).
rules.runtime_check: Tab through all submenu items; assert focus continues into subsequent page content without looping.

rules.rule: When focus moves outside the toggle + submenu (Tab away or click elsewhere), the submenu closes.
rules.runtime_check: With a submenu open, move focus outside it (Tab past it; separately click elsewhere); assert the submenu closes in both cases.

rules.rule: Esc closes an open submenu and returns focus to its toggle control.
rules.runtime_check: With focus inside an open submenu, press Esc; assert the submenu closes and focus lands on its toggle.

rules.rule: If the navigation contains multiple sub-menus, opening one closes any other open sub-menu.
rules.runtime_check: Open submenu A, then open submenu B; assert A closes (aria-expanded="false", content hidden) when B opens.

rules.rule: Do not open sub-menus on hover only.
rules.runtime_check: Without using a pointer, open each submenu via keyboard on its toggle; assert click/keyboard opening works — hover must not be the only path.

rules.rule: If hover-to-open is supported for mouse users, sub-menus must also be operable via click and keyboard, and keyboard users must not be forced to tab through submenu items unless they intentionally open them.
rules.runtime_check: Tab through the nav without activating toggles; assert closed-submenu items are skipped. Then open via keyboard and via click; assert both work.

rules.rule: Do not make a top-level item both a navigation link and the submenu toggle using the same element.
rules.static_hint: Flag an <a href> that also carries aria-expanded/submenu-toggle handlers.
rules.runtime_check: Activate the parent element; assert one element cannot both navigate and toggle — navigation and toggle are separate controls.

rules.rule: If the parent has its own destination page and also has a submenu, use parent link + separate toggle button.
rules.static_hint: Parents with destinations render as <a> plus an adjacent toggle <button>.
rules.llm_eval_question: For each parent that has a real destination page, is the markup split into a link plus a separate toggle button (case B)?

rules.rule: Do not use `role="menu"` / `role="menuitem"` unless you implement the full ARIA menu widget behavior (managed focus, arrow keys, typeahead).
rules.static_hint: Flag menu/menuitem roles in the navigation markup.
rules.runtime_check: If menu roles are present, exercise the full menu keyboard contract (roving focus, arrows, type-ahead); any missing behavior fails the rule.

rules.rule: Do not add `aria-haspopup` to the toggle; the submenu is a list of links, not a menu, so it announces a widget that is not present.
rules.static_hint: Flag aria-haspopup on submenu toggles.

rules.rule: Do not leave submenu content visible while `aria-expanded="false"` (and vice versa).
rules.runtime_check: Across open/close cycles, assert each submenu's visibility and its toggle's aria-expanded never disagree.

rules.rule: Do not strand focus by removing the currently focused submenu item without closing and allowing focus to move naturally.
rules.runtime_check: Close a submenu while focus is inside it; assert focus is not stranded on <body> — it lands on the toggle or the natural next element.

rules.rule: Do not render sub-menus expanded/visible by default. They open only after the user activates the corresponding toggle.
rules.static_hint: Initial render has all submenus hidden and toggles at aria-expanded="false".
rules.runtime_check: Load the page with no interaction; assert every submenu is hidden until its toggle is activated.

rules.rule: Use a native `<button>` (preferred), or `role="button"` only when a native button cannot be used.
rules.static_hint: Trigger is a native <button>, or role="button" as documented fallback.
rules.llm_eval_question: If role="button" is used, is there a genuine constraint that prevented a native <button>?

rules.rule: If `role="button"` is used instead of a native `<button>`, add `tabindex="0"`
rules.static_hint: Every role="button" trigger also carries tabindex="0".

rules.rule: If `role="button"` is used instead of a native `<button>`, add keyboard support for Enter and Space.
rules.static_hint: role="button" trigger has key handlers covering Enter and Space.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the role="button" trigger, press Enter, then Space; assert each toggles the dropdown.

rules.rule: ensuring Space prevents page scrolling while activating the control
rules.runtime_check: On a scrollable page, press Space on the focused role="button" trigger; assert activation occurs and the page does not scroll.

rules.rule: The list is shown/hidden in the DOM (e.g., via the `hidden` attribute), so that when closed, its content cannot be reached by keyboard or screen readers.
rules.static_hint: Closed state uses `hidden`/conditional rendering/display:none.
rules.runtime_check: With the dropdown closed, assert its links are unreachable via Tab and absent from the accessibility tree.

rules.rule:
Items are contained in a list structure (`
  • …
`) holding links and an optional trailing action `
rules.static_hint: Dropdown content is <a> elements (plus at most a trailing <button>) inside <li> children of a <ul>.

rules.rule: Any links and buttons comply with the rules for the `link.basic` and `button.basic` patterns.
rules.static_hint: Links have href; buttons are native; icon-only controls have accessible names; icons are aria-hidden.
rules.llm_eval_question: Do the dropdown's links and buttons individually satisfy the link.basic and button.basic Must Haves?
rules.runtime_check: Spot-check native behaviors: Enter activates links (Space does not); Enter/Space activate the trailing button.

rules.rule: The button has an accessible name that describes its purpose or action.
rules.static_hint: Trigger has a non-empty accessible name source.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the trigger's name convey what the dropdown contains (e.g., "Account"), rather than being generic?

rules.rule: For an icon-only avatar button, provide an accessible name using `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`.
rules.static_hint: Icon/avatar-only triggers carry a non-empty aria-label or resolving aria-labelledby.

rules.rule: Icons within the button are decorative (`aria-hidden="true"`).
rules.static_hint: Icon elements inside the trigger carry aria-hidden="true".

rules.rule: The button reflects open state with `aria-expanded="true|false"`.
rules.static_hint: Trigger carries aria-expanded bound to dropdown state.
rules.runtime_check: Open and close the dropdown; assert aria-expanded always matches actual list visibility.

rules.rule: The button is associated with the list container via `aria-controls="IDREF"`.
rules.static_hint: Trigger's aria-controls IDREF resolves to the list container's id.

rules.rule: The button does not carry `aria-haspopup`. A list of links is not a menu, listbox, tree, grid, or dialog, so `aria-haspopup` announces a widget that is not present; `aria-expanded` alone is the correct signal.
rules.static_hint: No aria-haspopup attribute on the trigger.

rules.rule: When the dropdown opens, focus remains on the invoking button.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dropdown; assert document.activeElement is still the trigger.

rules.rule: Keyboard users reach the first item with Tab.
rules.runtime_check: Open the dropdown, press Tab; assert focus lands on the first item in the list.

rules.rule: The dropdown does not trap focus. Users can Tab through the items and continue to the rest of the page.
rules.runtime_check: Tab through all dropdown items; assert focus continues into subsequent page content without looping.

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the invoking button and every item inside the list.
rules.static_hint: No outline suppression without :focus-visible replacement on the trigger or items.
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus the trigger and each item; assert a visible focus indicator renders on each.

rules.rule: When focus moves outside the button and list (Tab away, click elsewhere), the dropdown closes.
rules.runtime_check: With the dropdown open, Tab past its last item (and separately click elsewhere); assert the dropdown closes in both cases.

rules.rule: Esc closes the dropdown and returns focus to the invoking button (use `requestAnimationFrame` for the focus restore).
rules.static_hint: Esc handler restores trigger focus inside requestAnimationFrame.
rules.runtime_check: With focus inside the open dropdown, press Esc; assert it closes and document.activeElement becomes the trigger.

rules.rule: Do not use `role="menu"` or `role="menuitem"`; the items are links in a list, not menu commands.
rules.static_hint: Flag menu/menuitem roles anywhere in the dropdown.

rules.rule: Do not add `aria-haspopup` to the button; it announces a menu that is not there.
rules.static_hint: Flag aria-haspopup on the trigger.

rules.rule: Do not render the button without `aria-expanded`; state alone is what conveys open or closed.
rules.static_hint: Trigger must carry aria-expanded; flag its absence.

rules.rule: Do not make hover or pointer click the only way to open the dropdown; opening must also work with keyboard Enter and Space.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the trigger and press Enter, then Space; assert each opens the dropdown without any pointer input.

rules.rule: Do not leave the list visible while `aria-expanded="false"` (and vice versa).
rules.runtime_check: Across open/close cycles, assert list visibility and the trigger's aria-expanded never disagree.

rules.rule: Do not close the dropdown in a way that strands focus (e.g., removing the focused element without moving focus).
rules.runtime_check: Close the dropdown while focus is on one of its items; assert focus is not stranded on <body>.

rules.rule: Do not let a hidden dropdown be the only path to a destination; expose those links elsewhere on the site as well.
rules.llm_eval_question: Are the dropdown's destinations reachable through at least one other discoverable route (footer, settings page, sitemap), or is this collapsed list the sole path?

rules.rule: Provide a visible label for the field. Prefer a native `<label>` and ensure the custom UI is programmatically associated with the label (see `aria-labelledby` below).
rules.static_hint: A <label> element exists and its id is referenced by the button's aria-labelledby.
rules.runtime_check: Assert the label is rendered visible (not hidden or offscreen).

rules.rule: Include a visually hidden native `<select>` (required).
rules.static_hint: Markup contains a native <select> styled visually hidden alongside the custom UI.

rules.rule: The hidden `<select>` includes the same options and current value as the custom UI.
rules.static_hint: Both controls render from the same options data source.
rules.runtime_check: Compare the hidden select's <option> list and value against the custom UI's options and displayed value; change the selection and assert they stay equal.

rules.rule: The hidden `<select>` includes form attributes as needed (e.g., `name`, `required`, `disabled`).
rules.static_hint: Hidden select carries name (and required/disabled when the field's contract calls for them).
rules.llm_eval_question: Given the field's form behavior, are the appropriate form attributes present on the hidden select?

rules.rule: The hidden `<select>` is not focusable (so users don't tab to both controls).
rules.static_hint: Hidden select carries tabindex="-1" (or an equivalent focus exclusion).
rules.runtime_check: Tab through the field; assert only the custom button receives focus, never the hidden select.

rules.rule: The visible interactive control is a native `<button>` (preferred) or `role="button"` only when a native button cannot be used.
rules.static_hint: Visible trigger is a native <button>, or role="button" as documented fallback.
rules.llm_eval_question: If role="button" is used, is there a genuine constraint that prevented a native <button>?

rules.rule: If using `role="button"`, add `tabindex="0"`
rules.static_hint: role="button" trigger also carries tabindex="0".

rules.rule: If using `role="button"`, add keyboard support for Enter and Space.
rules.static_hint: role="button" trigger has key handlers covering Enter and Space.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the role="button" trigger, press Enter, then Space; assert each opens the listbox.

rules.rule: ensuring Space prevents page scrolling while activating.
rules.runtime_check: On a scrollable page, press Space on the focused role="button" trigger; assert activation occurs and the page does not scroll.

rules.rule: The button must expose "opens a listbox" semantics and state: `aria-haspopup="listbox"`
rules.static_hint: Button carries aria-haspopup with the exact value "listbox".

rules.rule: `aria-expanded="true|false"` reflecting open/closed
rules.static_hint: Button carries aria-expanded bound to popup state.
rules.runtime_check: Open and close the listbox; assert aria-expanded always matches actual visibility.

rules.rule: `aria-controls="IDREF"` pointing to the listbox element
rules.static_hint: Button's aria-controls IDREF resolves to the role="listbox" element.

rules.rule: The button must have an accessible name that includes the field label and current value.
rules.static_hint: Button's name computation draws on both the label element and a current-value element.
rules.runtime_check: Compute the button's accessible name; assert it contains the field label followed by the currently selected value, and updates when the value changes.

rules.rule: Recommended: `aria-labelledby="{labelId} {valueId}"` where `labelId` references the label element and `valueId` references an element containing the currently selected value text
rules.static_hint: Button's aria-labelledby lists the label element's id first, then the value element's id, both resolving.

rules.rule: The popup contains one element with `role="listbox"` and a stable ID (the same one referenced by `aria-controls`).
rules.static_hint: Exactly one role="listbox" element with an id matching the button's aria-controls.

rules.rule: Each option uses `role="option"` and has a stable ID.
rules.static_hint: Every option carries role="option" and a deterministic id usable by aria-activedescendant.

rules.rule: Each option exposes selection with `aria-selected="true|false"`.
rules.static_hint: Every option carries aria-selected bound to selection state.
rules.runtime_check: Change the selection; assert aria-selected="true" moves to the newly selected option only.

rules.rule: When the listbox is open, the "currently active" option is programmatically indicated (recommended). Preferred approach: keep focus on the button and set `aria-activedescendant="{optionId}"` on the button to point to the active option.
rules.static_hint: Button carries aria-activedescendant bound to the active option's id while open.
rules.runtime_check: Open the listbox and arrow through options; assert DOM focus stays on the button and aria-activedescendant always resolves to the highlighted option.

rules.rule: The listbox must be shown/hidden in the DOM so that when closed it cannot be reached by keyboard or screen readers (e.g., via `hidden`).
rules.static_hint: Closed state uses `hidden`/conditional rendering/display:none.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox closed, assert its options are unreachable and absent from the accessibility tree.

rules.rule: Tab / Shift+Tab moves focus to/from the button like a normal form field (no focus trap).
rules.runtime_check: Tab into and out of the field in both directions; assert focus moves like a native form field with no trapping.

rules.rule: Enter or Space on the button opens the listbox.
rules.runtime_check: Press Enter (and separately Space) on the focused button; assert the listbox opens.

rules.rule: When open: Arrow Up/Down moves the active option.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox open, press Arrow Down/Up; assert the active option advances/retreats one option per press.

rules.rule: When open: Enter or Space selects the active option and closes the listbox.
rules.runtime_check: Press Enter (and separately Space) on an active option; assert it becomes selected, the listbox closes, and the button's value text updates.

rules.rule: When open: Esc closes the listbox and returns the user to the button state.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox open, press Esc; assert it closes without changing the selection and focus remains on the button.

rules.rule: Clicking/tapping outside closes the listbox.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox open, click outside the component; assert the listbox closes.

rules.rule: The label and current value are announced together (via `aria-labelledby`).
rules.static_hint: aria-labelledby composes label + value elements.
rules.runtime_check: Compute the button's accessible name; assert it contains both the label and the current value.

rules.rule: Expanded/collapsed state is announced (via `aria-expanded`).
rules.static_hint: aria-expanded present on the button.
rules.runtime_check: Assert the button's expanded state in the accessibility tree tracks the popup's open/closed state.

rules.rule: The user can perceive which option is selected (`aria-selected`).
rules.static_hint: aria-selected present on options.
rules.runtime_check: Assert the selected option's aria-selected="true" matches the visible selection.

rules.rule: The user can perceive which option is currently active when navigating (`aria-activedescendant` recommended).
rules.static_hint: aria-activedescendant (or equivalent focus indication) present while navigating.
rules.runtime_check: Arrow through options; assert the active option is programmatically exposed at each step.

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the button that opens the select, and all option elements inside.
rules.static_hint: No outline/highlight suppression without replacement on the button and options.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the button and navigate options; assert visible indicators render on the button and on the active option.

rules.rule: Do not omit the visually hidden native `<select>` when this control participates in form submission or should support autofill.
rules.static_hint: If the component sits in a <form> or has a name-able value, a hidden native <select> must be present.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does this select participate in form submission or autofill, and if so, is the hidden native select present?

rules.rule: Do not implement the trigger as a non-interactive element (e.g., `<div>`) without button semantics.
rules.static_hint: Flag click handlers on trigger elements lacking <button> or role="button"+tabindex.

rules.rule: Do not use `role="menu"` / `role="menuitem"` for select options.
rules.static_hint: Flag menu/menuitem roles in the popup; require listbox/option.

rules.rule: Do not make each option tabbable (avoid forcing users to Tab through options).
rules.static_hint: Flag tabindex="0" on option elements.
rules.runtime_check: With the listbox open, press Tab; assert focus leaves the component rather than stepping through options.

rules.rule: Do not leave the listbox visible while `aria-expanded="false"` (and vice versa).
rules.runtime_check: Across open/close cycles, assert listbox visibility and the button's aria-expanded never disagree.

rules.rule: Do not allow the custom UI and hidden `<select>` to get out of sync (options/value must match).
rules.runtime_check: After every selection change (keyboard and pointer), assert the hidden select's value and options equal the custom UI's.

rules.rule: Use a native `<select>` element with native `<option>` elements.
rules.static_hint: Control is a native <select> containing native <option> children.

rules.rule: Provide a visible label associated with the `<select>` via a native `<label>` using `for`/`id`.
rules.static_hint: A <label for> matches the select's id.
rules.runtime_check: Assert the label is rendered visible.

rules.rule: The `<select>` participates in the form via a `name` attribute
rules.static_hint: Select carries a non-empty name attribute.

rules.rule: The `<select>` uses native attributes (`required`, `disabled`) as needed.
rules.static_hint: Required/disabled states use the native attributes, not aria substitutes.
rules.llm_eval_question: Given the field's contract, are the appropriate native attributes applied (and no ARIA re-implementations)?

rules.rule: Keep option content as plain text.
rules.static_hint: No markup (images, spans with styling hooks, controls) inside <option> elements.

rules.rule: Style the control with CSS only (`appearance` and related properties).
rules.static_hint: Styling is CSS on the native select; no JS re-implementation of open/close/selection behavior.

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the `<select>` control.
rules.static_hint: No outline suppression without :focus-visible replacement on the select.
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus the select; assert a visible focus indicator renders.

rules.rule: Do not rebuild the select with `<div>` elements and scripting when a native `<select>` suffices.
rules.static_hint: Flag div-based dropdown widgets replacing a native select.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does this use case genuinely require a custom widget (rich option content, etc.), or would the native select suffice?

rules.rule: Do not remove the native element in favor of a custom widget for styling reasons alone.
rules.static_hint: Detect replacement of the native select as the review trigger.
rules.llm_eval_question: Is the only motivation for the custom widget visual styling — something CSS on the native select could achieve?

rules.rule: Do not replace native behavior with scripting.
rules.static_hint: Flag JS handlers that preventDefault or re-implement the select's open/navigate/select behaviors.
rules.runtime_check: Operate the select with keyboard and pointer; assert native behaviors (open, arrow, type-ahead, select) work unmodified.

rules.rule: Do not use the `multiple` attribute (native multi-select tests very poorly).
rules.static_hint: Flag the multiple attribute on the select.

rules.rule: Do not hide the label.
rules.static_hint: Flag sr-only/display:none/visibility:hidden styles on the select's label.
rules.runtime_check: Assert the label is rendered visible in the layout.

rules.rule: The switch has `role="switch"`.
rules.static_hint: Control carries role="switch" (on the input, button, or div implementation).

rules.rule: The switch has a visible text label, either programmatically associated with the control (`label[for]`) or matched by an accessible name on the control (`aria-label` or `aria-labelledby`).
rules.static_hint: A label mechanism is present: label[for], a resolving aria-labelledby, aria-label, or a wrapping label.
rules.runtime_check: Compute the switch's accessible name; assert it is non-empty and derives from the visible label.

rules.rule: The accessible name begins with the visible label text, and may append a short amount of additional context for screen reader users (via `aria-label`, `aria-labelledby`, or offscreen `.sr-only` text).
rules.static_hint: Any aria-label begins with the visible label text.
rules.llm_eval_question: If context is appended beyond the visible label, is it brief and clarifying rather than verbose or redundant?
rules.runtime_check: Compute the accessible name; assert it begins with the visible label text.

rules.rule: The accessible name describes the setting the switch controls, worded so it is true when the switch is on (e.g., "Enable notifications").
rules.static_hint: Switch has a non-empty accessible name source.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the name describe the setting being controlled, phrased so it reads true when on (e.g., "Enable notifications"), rather than vague or stateful ("On")?

rules.rule: The switch's on/off state is exposed via `aria-checked` (`"true"`/`"false"`), or via the native `checked` property when the switch is an `input[type="checkbox"]`.
rules.static_hint: The switch carries aria-checked, or (for input[type=checkbox]) uses the native checked property; exactly one of the two conveys state.

rules.rule: The exposed state stays in sync with the switch as it toggles.
rules.runtime_check: Toggle the switch; assert aria-checked (or native checked) flips in sync with the visible state each time.

rules.rule: The switch is focusable so Tab and Shift+Tab reach it: a native `input[type="checkbox"]` is focusable by default; a non-native element (`div`/`button` with `role="switch"`) must include `tabIndex="0"`.
rules.static_hint: Switch is a natively focusable element, or a non-native element carrying tabindex="0".
rules.runtime_check: Tab and Shift+Tab through the page; assert the switch receives focus in both directions.

rules.rule: Space toggles the switch.
rules.static_hint: Native control, or a keydown handler covering Space is attached to the switch element.
rules.llm_eval_question: Tracing the key handler, does pressing Space toggle the switch state (and preventDefault the page scroll)?
rules.runtime_check: Focus the switch and press Space; assert the state toggles.

rules.rule: Enter toggles the switch. Exception: a native `input[type="checkbox"]` toggles with Space only, not Enter.
rules.static_hint: For non-checkbox implementations a keydown handler covering Enter is attached; checkbox implementations correctly omit Enter handling.
rules.llm_eval_question: Tracing the handler, does Enter toggle for non-checkbox switches, and is Enter correctly not required for input[type=checkbox]?
rules.runtime_check: For non-checkbox implementations press Enter and assert toggle; for checkbox implementations assert Space toggles and Enter is not required.

rules.rule: If multiple switches are presented as a labeled set, group them with `fieldset` + `legend` or `role="group"` + `aria-labelledby`.
rules.static_hint: A related set of switches is wrapped in fieldset+legend or role="group"+aria-labelledby.
rules.llm_eval_question: Do these switches form a related set, and if so is the group structure present?

rules.rule: The group's label describes the purpose of the set (WCAG 2.4.6).
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the group's legend or aria-label describe the purpose of the switch set, rather than being generic or missing?

rules.rule: Associate any additional descriptive static text with the switch via `aria-describedby` on the switch element itself. Do not place `aria-describedby` on a `fieldset` or group container, which screen readers announce unreliably; put group-level context in the `legend`.
rules.static_hint: Any aria-describedby is on the switch element (a focusable control) with a resolving id; flag aria-describedby placed on a fieldset/group container.
rules.llm_eval_question: Is there rendered helper/description text for the switch that is not programmatically associated via aria-describedby on the switch?

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the switch control.
rules.static_hint: No outline suppression without a :focus-visible replacement on the switch.
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus the switch; assert a visible focus indicator renders.

rules.rule: Do not use a switch for non-setting actions; use it only for persistent on/off settings.
rules.llm_eval_question: Does this control represent a persistent on/off setting, or is it being used for a one-shot action or navigation (which should be a button)?

rules.rule: Do not use both `checked` and `aria-checked` on `input[type="checkbox"]`.
rules.static_hint: Flag a checkbox input that carries both the native checked property and aria-checked.

rules.rule: The live region container must be present in the DOM when the page/view loads.
rules.static_hint: Live region element is rendered unconditionally at mount, not created when a toast fires.
rules.runtime_check: Load the page with no toast triggered; assert the live region container already exists (empty) in the DOM.

rules.rule: The toast message must be announced via `role="status"`, or an equivalent such as `aria-live="polite"` and `aria-atomic="true"`.
rules.static_hint: Live region carries role="status", or aria-live="polite" plus aria-atomic="true".

rules.rule: When a toast is triggered, its message text must be injected into the existing live region container.
rules.runtime_check: Trigger a toast; assert the message text appears inside the pre-existing live region element (same node, not a new container).

rules.rule: The toast must not move focus automatically when it appears.
rules.runtime_check: Focus an element, trigger a toast; assert document.activeElement is unchanged.

rules.rule: The toast must disappear automatically (recommended ~5 seconds).
rules.runtime_check: Trigger a toast and wait past the timeout; assert the toast is no longer visible without user action.

rules.rule: The live region text must be cleared when the toast dismisses to avoid stale messages being discovered later.
rules.runtime_check: After the toast dismisses, assert the live region container's text content is empty.

rules.rule: If a dismiss button is present, it must not steal focus when the toast appears.
rules.runtime_check: Focus an element, trigger a toast with a dismiss button; assert focus does not move to the dismiss button.

rules.rule: Do not mount/unmount the live region container based on toast visibility.
rules.static_hint: Flag conditional rendering of the live region element tied to toast state.
rules.runtime_check: Trigger and dismiss toasts; assert the live region element is the same persistent DOM node throughout.

rules.rule: Do not move focus into the toast when it appears.
rules.runtime_check: Trigger a toast; assert document.activeElement never becomes the toast or a descendant of it.

rules.rule: Do not use `role="alertdialog"`.
rules.static_hint: Flag role="alertdialog" on the toast or its container.

rules.rule: The toast must not contain buttons or elements that require user action (use `snackbar` or `dialog` instead).
rules.static_hint: Flag interactive elements inside the toast (an optional dismiss button per the Must Haves is the only tolerated control).
rules.llm_eval_question: Does the toast's content demand a user decision or action (confirm, undo, input), meaning it should be a snackbar or dialog instead?

rules.rule: The tooltip bubble is an element with `role="tooltip"` and a stable `id`.
rules.static_hint: Tooltip element carries role="tooltip" and a deterministic id.

rules.rule: The trigger references the tooltip with `aria-describedby` pointing to the tooltip's `id`.
rules.static_hint: Trigger's aria-describedby IDREF equals the tooltip's id.

rules.rule: The tooltip supplements the accessible name, it does not replace it. The trigger has its own accessible name (e.g., visible text or `aria-label`).
rules.static_hint: Trigger has a non-empty accessible name independent of the tooltip; tooltip is referenced by aria-describedby, not aria-labelledby.
rules.llm_eval_question: Is the tooltip content supplemental description rather than serving as the trigger's only name?

rules.rule: The tooltip appears on both pointer hover and keyboard focus of the trigger.
rules.runtime_check: Hover the trigger; assert the tooltip shows. Separately, keyboard-focus the trigger; assert the tooltip shows.

rules.rule: Esc dismisses the tooltip whether it was opened by pointer hover or by keyboard focus, and does not move pointer hover or keyboard focus (dismissible).
rules.runtime_check: Show the tooltip via hover, press Esc; assert it hides. Repeat via focus; assert it hides and document.activeElement is unchanged.

rules.rule: Bind the Esc handler at the document level, not only on the trigger, so it works when the tooltip is shown by hover and the trigger does not hold focus.
rules.static_hint: Esc keydown listener is attached to document while the tooltip is visible, not solely to the trigger element.
rules.runtime_check: With focus elsewhere on the page, hover the trigger and press Esc; assert the tooltip dismisses.

rules.rule: When the trigger has keyboard focus, the trigger keeps focus after dismissal.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the trigger, press Esc to dismiss; assert document.activeElement is still the trigger.

rules.rule: The tooltip remains visible while the pointer is over the trigger or over the tooltip, and while the trigger has focus (hoverable and persistent).
rules.runtime_check: Move the pointer from the trigger onto the tooltip bubble; assert it stays visible. Hold keyboard focus on the trigger; assert it stays visible.

rules.rule: Track hover and focus as independent conditions and keep the tooltip open while either is active, so a stray pointer movement does not hide it while the trigger still has keyboard focus.
rules.static_hint: Visibility state derives from hover OR focus flags, not a single shared boolean.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the trigger, then move the pointer across and off the trigger; assert the tooltip remains visible while focus is held.

rules.rule: The tooltip is not focusable and contains plain text only, with no interactive content.
rules.static_hint: Tooltip has no tabindex and no interactive descendants; content is text-only.
rules.runtime_check: Tab through the page with the tooltip visible; assert the tooltip never receives focus.

rules.rule: Ensure a visible focus state (e.g., a 2px solid outline offset by 1-2px) around the trigger.
rules.static_hint: No outline suppression without :focus-visible replacement on the trigger.
rules.runtime_check: Keyboard-focus the trigger; assert a visible focus indicator renders.

rules.rule: Do not place interactive content (links, buttons, form controls) in a tooltip.
rules.static_hint: Flag any interactive element inside the role="tooltip" element.

rules.rule: Do not rely on the native `title` attribute as the tooltip; it is not keyboard-accessible and is announced inconsistently.
rules.static_hint: Flag title attributes used as the tooltip mechanism on the trigger.

rules.rule: Do not make the tooltip vanish when the pointer moves from the trigger toward the tooltip; it must be hoverable.
rules.runtime_check: Traverse the pointer from trigger to tooltip across the gap; assert the tooltip does not hide during the traversal or while hovered.

rules.rule: Do not show the tooltip on hover only; it must also appear on keyboard focus.
rules.runtime_check: Without any pointer input, keyboard-focus the trigger; assert the tooltip appears.

rules.rule: Do not hide the tooltip on `onMouseLeave` while the trigger still has keyboard focus; hover and focus must be tracked independently.
rules.static_hint: Flag mouseleave handlers that hide unconditionally without checking the focus flag.
rules.runtime_check: Focus the trigger, hover it, then move the pointer away; assert the tooltip stays visible while focus remains.

rules.rule: Do not use `role="menu"` on the tooltip.
rules.static_hint: Flag role="menu" on the tooltip element.

rules.rule: Do not leave the tooltip visible after the trigger has lost focus and the pointer has left both the trigger and the tooltip.
rules.runtime_check: Blur the trigger and move the pointer off both trigger and tooltip; assert the tooltip hides.
